mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount rate on equity cash flows declines and risk appetite improves, so the S&P 500 should post positive forward returns over the next month. The signal fails if rate cuts occur only after growth or credit stress has already begun to dominate equity pricing.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines are followed by negative 20d SPX returns in most observations, or the sign of the relationship reverses during easing periods, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS above its recent trend implies stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which should lift near-term S&P 500 returns through higher expected earnings. The signal fails when sales strength is offset by tightening financial conditions or margin compression.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS breakouts do not produce positive 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional return distribution is indistinguishable from baseline over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deterioration in labor conditions, measured by rising UNRATE and slowing PAYEMS, signals weakening household income and demand, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The mechanism fails if labor weakness is fully offset by simultaneous policy easing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months and PAYEMS growth turns negative
failure_condition: Reject if labor-deterioration triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the combined signal has no incremental explanatory power versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration combined with a high policy rate implies tightening real financial conditions and higher equity discount rates, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if inflation rises while the market interprets it as growth-confirming and rates remain anchored.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rises above its 12-month average while FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/high-rate episodes do not generate negative 20d SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the interaction term is not significant out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and inventory rebuilding, which should support cyclical earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over a one-quarter horizon. The mechanism fails if output gains are driven by transitory restocking without demand confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is not associated with positive 60d SPX returns, or if the rolling sign of the relationship is unstable across recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in housing starts signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and lower future consumption, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month. The effect fails when lower housing starts merely reflect supply constraints rather than demand weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST declines are not followed by negative 20d SPX returns more often than chance, or if the effect disappears once broad market volatility is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth is positive and UNRATE is stable or falling, labor income and consumption remain resilient, supporting earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails if employment gains are concentrated in low-wage sectors or lag aggregate demand.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises while UNRATE does not increase over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined employment-stability condition does not improve 60d SPX returns relative to unconditional returns, or if positive signals vanish in out-of-sample rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad demand acceleration, which should raise forward earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if GDP gains are revised away or retail sales strength is concentrated in inflation rather than real activity.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP-and-sales co-acceleration does not outperform baseline in 60d SPX returns, or if one series alone explains the effect and the interaction adds no incremental signal.
